name: Guaviare (Colombia)

colors.symbolism: the Amazonian rainforest covering most of Guaviare
peace and the integrity of the people; also elements of the central coat of arms
the rivers, in particular the Guaviare river that gives the department its name; also elements of the coat of arms

description: Three equal horizontal stripes of green, white, and blue, with the coat of arms of Guaviare placed at the centre.
history: Guaviare was created as a Comisaria in 1977 and elevated to a department by the 1991 Constitution. The flag's three stripes evoke the rainforest, the Guaviare River, and the peace of its people.

features.symbolism: green for the Amazonian rainforest, white for the peace and integrity of its people, and blue for the rivers and the wealth of waters
the department's coat of arms, featuring elements of the Amazonian environment
